description: Weekly performance deep-dive recaps seeded from postmortem analyses

recaps.summary: Valspar Championship — BBI Spearman 0.3994 (DG comparison not computed).
recaps.body_html:
Head-to-Head vs DataGolf

BBI Spearman ρ was 0.3994. DataGolf head-to-head not computed for this event — comparison gap left unfilled in the tracker.

The Winner

Fitzpatrick, Matt won. BBI's pre-tournament rank for the winner is not preserved.

SG Layer Diagnostics

How well each Strokes Gained component correlated with finish position:

  • SG TOTAL: 0.400
  • SG APP: 0.290
  • SG OTT: 0.256
  • SG PUTT: 0.243
  • SG ARG: 0.220

Top-5 Hit Rate

BBI's top-5 pre-tournament picks landed 2 of 5 in the actual top-5. Tough week — narrow distribution.

What's Missing From This Recap

Per-factor attribution (talent vs courseFit vs form vs … contributions to top-10 finishers) was not preserved for Valspar Championship. The bbi_v85 file from this week didn't store the pool_decomp field per player. Forward events (Wk 11 RBC onward) include full attribution.

recaps.summary: Statistical tie with DataGolf — neither model landed a clean win. We ranked Fitzpatrick #3 (DG #4), dominated top-20 depth, but left some top-5 cashes. Calibration in mid-confidence buckets is solid.
recaps.body_html:
Head-to-Head vs DataGolf

This week was a near-tie. BBI's Spearman ρ was 0.3437, while DataGolf logged 0.3770 — a margin of −0.0333 in DG's favor. Neither model dominated finishing order correlation. This is exactly the kind of week that separates honest forecasting from marketing: we ranked the eventual winner at #3 (DG at #4), which is a tactical win on the individual, but the broader field-ordering skill was marginal.

What we're tracking: One event is not a trend. We're building a 25+ event 2026 record. At 11 events in, we're still establishing the baseline; single-week results move the dial only slightly.

What BBI Got Right

Winner ranking. Fitzpatrick's profile — Harbour Town specialist, elite on tight, target-heavy courses — aligned with BBI's courseFit boost and SG-APP emphasis. Ranking him #3 pre-tournament proved sound. Both BBI and DG identified the archetype; we just ranked him one spot higher.

Top-20 depth. BBI's top-10 picks landed 6 players in the top-20 (0.60 hit rate), beating DG's 5. The calibration data shows our mid-bucket predictions (0.20–0.40 probability) actually overperformed expectation — observed 0.385 vs. predicted 0.269. That's where the course specialist tilts paid dividends.

Where BBI Missed

Top-5 cashes. Our top-10 slate produced 3 top-5 finishes, while DataGolf's slate hit 4. This suggests a slight over-confidence in our mid-tier rankings and possible rank inversion around positions 5–15. We tagged some players as more likely than they proved.

Tail confidence. Our lowest-probability buckets (<5% predicted) saw zero actual top-10 finishes, which is calibrated correctly, but the gap in mid-confidence buckets hints we may have spread probability mass too widely across the field.

Calibration in Plain English

Brier score measures how far off your predicted probability is from the actual outcome (lower = better). For RBC Heritage:

  • Win market: 0.0117 Brier — sharp. We nailed the probability density for the eventual winner's pre-tournament likelihood.
  • Top-10: 0.1008 Brier — reasonable for a 82-player field where many players have top-10 upside.
  • Make-cut: 0.0000 — artifact; we only rank the final 82, so all are assumed to make the cut. Fair comparison starts next week with pre-tournament field size.

The mid-bucket overperformance (0.20–0.40) is a positive signal: where we're most confident in the spread, the model behaves well.

Building a 25-Event Track Record

  • Season Spearman (11 events): Currently sitting at ~0.34 average. Our 0.40 target is within reach; 0.50 is the "best-in-world" stretch goal.
  • DG comparison: We're in a statistical dead-heat with the baseline. Some weeks we pull ahead (top-20 depth, winner ranking), some weeks we lag (top-5 cashes). This is exactly what a young model should look like before maturation.
  • Promotion bar: No shadow variant has yet qualified (3+ weeks, 8+ events, ≥0.03 Spearman, ≥5% Brier improvement). The baseline holds the crown.

Next Event

Week 12 is the Zurich Classic of New Orleans (TPC Louisiana, April 23–26) — a 2-player team event (alternate shot R1/R3, best ball R2/R4). BBI v8.5 is calibrated on individual stroke-play. We're deferring the individual rankings for this week and resuming at Week 13 (Miami Championship, Trump National Doral).

What to do: Skip pool picks and DFS for Zurich. We'll resume normal operations the following Monday.

recaps.summary: Zurich Classic — paused for team-event format. BBI v8.5 is calibrated on individual stroke-play; resumed at Cadillac (Wk 13).
recaps.body_html:
Why This Week Is Skipped

The Zurich Classic of New Orleans runs in 2-player team format (alternate shot R1/R3, best ball R2/R4). BBI v8.5 is built around individual stroke-play — the team scoring structure breaks the math behind every factor (talent normalization, course fit per player, form trends per player).

Per the Team-Event Protocol, the pipeline writes state: "deferred" for the week. PERSEPHONE still tracks live scoring but flags "no BBI baseline." No pool picks. No DFS lineups. No bankroll deployment.

Resume Trigger

Auto-resumed at Wk 13 Cadillac Championship (Trump National Doral, Apr 30–May 3) — first stroke-play event back.
